method: each errors.append branch of a declared lifecycle model is disabled one at a time in an isolated copy; a guard is proved only when the suite fails without it
subject: tests/lifecycle-closure/check.py

branches.guard: grant trace does not start from aggregate absence/not_issued
grant trace is not single-use
step {} exposes a final grant use without immediate exhaustion
step {} uses a grant while {}
step {} exceeds max_uses
step {} exhausts before the exact final legal use
step {} cannot apply {} from {}
step {} uses unknown grant event {}
step {} declared_from does not equal folded state {}
step {} declared_to does not equal legal result {}
grant trace ends with an unexhausted final use
protocol origin fabricates or depends on a prior canonical state
protocol origin is not explicitly from aggregate absence
protocol origin does not materialize version 1
protocol origin lacks an exact frozen ProtocolSnapshot reference
protocol origin lacks exact source-draft evidence
protocol origin lacks one or more exact validation evidence references
protocol origin lacks an exact exposure-history digest
protocol origin lacks an exact integrity rule identity

protocol freeze is not at index 1 of one four-event commit
protocol grant-use/freeze/integrity/exhaustion order is not exact
protocol origin lacks {}

data-use payload use-event differs from authority evidence
data-use final grant use is not exhausted in the cohort

data-use grant-use/decision/exhaustion order is not exact

stale/completed are not explicitly projection-only labels

not_auditable.reason: returns refusal lists directly and delegates to schema_contract_errors instead of appending to a local errors list, so branch mutation cannot attribute its guards. Its coverage is unmeasured, not proved.

boundary: architecture evidence about retained bytes only; not a runtime, an admitted member, an independently reproduced verdict, or Gate A acceptance
